Actually "prevent a run of (+1) or more identical characters" means we want to reject passwords with more than X consecutive same characters; setting maxclassrepeat = X will reject passwords with > X consecutive same chars. So to prevent a run of 2 or more identical characters, set maxclassrepeat = 1? Wait: If maxclassrepeat = N, then passwords containing more than N consecutive identical characters are rejected. So to prevent a run of 2 or more identical characters, we set maxclassrepeat = 1 (reject >1 consecutive identical => reject any password with 2 or more same chars). But typical is to limit to 3? Actually many policies set maxclassrepeat = 3 to reject 4 or more same consecutive characters. The description says "prevent a run of ( + 1) or more identical characters." That seems ambiguous. Likely they want to set maxclassrepeat = 3 to prevent 4 or more. But we can follow the recommendation: The remediation script suggests: if grep -sq maxclassrepeat /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf ; then sed -i "/maxclassrepeat/d" /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf fi . That is to delete any existing maxclassrepeat lines then add a new line? Actually they didn't show adding. Possibly they intend to set it after removing.

We need to output the remediation steps as plain text, likely with commands.

Thus produce:

1. Check current value: grep -i maxclassrepeat /etc/security/pwquality.conf
2. If present, note value.
3. To enforce, set maxclassrepeat = 3 (or maybe 2). We'll decide to set to 3 as common.
4. Use sed to replace or add line.

We'll provide a remediation script:

```
# Determine desired maxclassrepeat value (e.g., 3)
DESIRED=3
# Backup original file
cp /etc/security/pwquality.conf /etc/security/pwquality.conf.bak
# Remove any existing maxclassrepeat lines
sed -i '/^\s*maxclassrepeat\s*=/d' /etc/security/pwquality.conf
# Ensure the line is added under the [default] section or at end
if ! grep -q '^\s*\[\s*default\s*\]' /etc/security/pwquality.conf; then
    sed -i '$a\[default\]' /etc/security/pwquality.conf
fi
# Append or set maxclassrepeat line
sed -i "/^\[default\]/a maxclassrepeat = $DESIRED" /etc/security/pwquality.conf
# Verify
grep maxclassrepeat /etc/security/pwquality.conf
```
